name: Adventure
description_short: Adventure Credit Union is a full-service Michigan credit union serving the Greater Grand Rapids area with checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and investment services.
description_long:
Adventure Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative based in Michigan that has been serving its community for 90 years. The institution operates multiple branches in the Greater Grand Rapids area and offers comprehensive financial products and services to its membership base. The credit union recently merged with Astera Credit Union, expanding its service capabilities and member base.

Adventure Credit Union provides a full range of banking and lending products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit, retirement accounts (IRAs), health savings accounts, and education savings accounts. On the lending side, they offer personal loans, auto loans (including electric and hybrid vehicles), mortgages, home equity loans and HELOCs, recreational vehicle loans, private student loans, debt consolidation loans, business loans, and equipment financing. The credit union also provides investment services, financial planning, retirement planning, and insurance products through partnerships.

The credit union distinguishes itself through its local commitment to the Michigan community, emphasizing member-first service and community involvement through their "Adventure Gives" program and financial wellness initiatives. They offer competitive rates, member-exclusive benefits like the $100 sign-up bonus, and specialized services such as SnapCash loans, business treasury management, and a mobile check deposit feature. Their routing number is 272480432, and they maintain digital banking options including online banking, mobile app banking, and video teller machines.

As a credit union, Adventure is governed by its members rather than external shareholders, theoretically aligning incentives toward member benefit rather than profit maximization. However, membership eligibility requirements apply, and their service area is primarily limited to Michigan. While they offer competitive CD rates (4.90% APY on 6-month CDs) and various loan products, consumers should verify membership eligibility and compare rates with other institutions before committing. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (currently 4.90% APY on 6-month terms)
Personal loans and debt consolidation loans
Business loans, business real estate loans, and business equipment financing
pros: Member-owned credit union structure prioritizes member interests over external shareholders
Competitive CD rates at 4.90% APY for 6-month certificates as of the 90th anniversary promotion
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and investment services under one institution
Specialized loan programs including electric/hybrid vehicle financing and Lansing-built vehicle loans
Digital banking conveniences including mobile check deposit, video teller machines, and online bill pay
Community commitment demonstrated through Adventure Gives program and financial wellness resources
$100 sign-up bonus for new members opening accounts
cons: Geographic limitation: primarily serves Greater Grand Rapids area and Michigan only
Membership eligibility requirements apply (not open to everyone unlike banks)
Limited information on website regarding specific membership eligibility criteria and requirements
Auto loan APR stated as low as 5.24%, which is competitive but not exceptionally low for credit unions
No information provided about monthly account fees, minimum balance requirements, or overdraft policies
best_for: Michigan residents in the Greater Grand Rapids area seeking full-service banking with local commitment
Credit union members wanting comprehensive products (checking, savings, loans, mortgages) from a single institution
Vehicle buyers interested in specialized financing including electric, hybrid, or RV loans
Small business owners needing business accounts, equipment financing, and treasury management services
diagnosis: Adventure Credit Union is best for Michigan residents in the Greater Grand Rapids area who value local, member-owned banking with comprehensive products and community commitment. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—membership and service availability are restricted to Michigan, making this unsuitable for out-of-state consumers or those outside the served territory.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
